skill.name: the-archivist
skill.description: Helps teams document engineering decisions during implementation using inline rationale, decision logs, and Architecture Decision Records.
skill.summary: Document engineering decisions with ADR-ready rationale and trade-off records.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk, but semantic review found only Markdown templates, ADR examples, and local review commands. The weak crypto, backtick execution, browser storage, network, reconnaissance, and critical heuristic alerts are false positives with no executable payload or exfiltration path. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged fenced Markdown blocks and inline code spans as Ruby or shell execution. The cited content is documentation templates and examples, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced Markdown examples, inline file paths, or documentation snippets. No runtime language file, command launcher, or user-controlled command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography alerts occur in ADR placeholders, headings, prose, and example decision text. No cryptographic API, hashing implementation, or authentication logic appears in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows these lines are templates for describing decisions and trade-offs. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, custom crypto, or credential handling code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Storage and Network Mentions
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The browser storage alert is an example of documenting a security decision. The network alert is the text axios-retry in a code review prompt example, not an HTTP client call.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines mention localStorage and axios-retry only as documentation examples. No browser API access, fetch call, external endpoint, or data transmission code exists in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance and Critical Heuristic
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance alert points to a rapid decision-capture section, and nearby shell examples are local audit commands for reviewing repository history. The critical combination is not confirmed because there is no credential access, active network call, or executable payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only shell commands shown are git log, ls, and grep examples for manual documentation audits. They do not collect secrets, contact a network service, or run automatically.

content.value_statement: Engineering decisions often lose rationale over time, causing teams to repeat mistakes. The Archivist captures context, alternatives, and trade-offs in ADR-ready documentation.
actual_capabilities: Classifies decisions into inline comments, brief decision log entries, or full ADRs.
Provides reusable templates for decision logs and Architecture Decision Records.
Prompts engineers to record context, alternatives, rationale, and consequences.
Adds review checklists for detecting missing decision documentation.
Guides teams to preserve accepted ADRs through superseding records.
limitations: It does not automatically inspect repositories or generate records without user direction.
It does not enforce documentation rules in CI or project management tools.
Its templates may need adaptation to existing team standards.
It cannot verify whether a documented rationale is factually complete.

use_cases.title: Record Architecture Choices
use_cases.description: Create full ADRs for framework, database, infrastructure, or service boundary decisions before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Explain Trade-offs During Delivery
use_cases.description: Capture why a feature uses one library, data model, or performance strategy instead of reasonable alternatives.

use_cases.title: Review Missing Rationale
use_cases.description: Check pull requests for undocumented technology choices, convention breaks, and non-obvious implementation decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Inline Rationale
prompt_templates.prompt: I chose this implementation approach over another option. Help me write a short inline decision note with the reason and rejected alternative.

prompt_templates.title: Create Decision Log Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this engineering choice into a brief DECISIONS.md entry with context, decision, rationale, alternatives, and consequences.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Full ADR
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full Architecture Decision Record for this system-level decision. Include drivers, considered options, outcome, consequences, and related decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Change Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these planned changes for engineering decisions that need documentation. Classify each one as inline, decision log, or full ADR.

output_examples.input: We chose PostgreSQL instead of MongoDB for the account service.
output_examples.output: A brief decision entry with the service context, the selected database, rationale for relational consistency, rejected alternatives, and operational consequences.

output_examples.input: This recursive tree traversal may surprise reviewers.
output_examples.output: An inline rationale that explains readability, names the iterative stack alternative, and records the accepted memory trade-off.

output_examples.input: Review this feature plan for undocumented technical choices.
output: Full ADR recommended for the new queue provider.
Brief decision entry recommended for the cache invalidation strategy.
Inline rationale recommended for the custom retry behavior.

best_practices: Document decisions while making them, before implementation details obscure the original context.
Match the documentation depth to the decision impact and reversal cost.
Always record rejected alternatives so future engineers understand the trade-off.
anti_patterns: Do not write ADRs after the fact only to justify choices already made.
Do not create full ADRs for trivial naming, formatting, or standard framework conventions.
Do not edit accepted ADR history; create a superseding record when decisions change.

faq.question: When should I use a full ADR?
faq.answer: Use a full ADR for architecture decisions, hard-to-reverse choices, new core dependencies, or decisions that affect multiple teams.

faq.question: When is an inline note enough?
faq.answer: Use an inline note for local choices that are easy to reverse but may confuse a future maintainer.

faq.question: Does this skill generate documentation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides prompts, templates, and review checks that the user applies during implementation and review.

faq.question: Can teams use their own ADR format?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides a MADR-inspired format, but its decision triggers work with other ADR templates.

faq.question: How does it help code review?
faq.answer: It gives reviewers a checklist for new dependencies, architecture choices, convention breaks, and missing rationale.

faq.question: Should old ADRs be updated?
faq.answer: Accepted ADRs should preserve history. Create a new record that supersedes the old decision when the direction changes.
